steps.text: Determine the rotation angles using the alternative measurement site selection procedure.
Use a directional flow-sensing probe to measure pitch and yaw angles of the gas flow at 40 or more traverse points.
Calculate the resultant angle and compare it with acceptable criteria for mean and standard deviation.
Use a minimum of 40 traverse points for circular ducts and 42 points for rectangular ducts for the gas flow angle determinations.
Follow the procedure outlined in section 11.3 and table 1–1 or 1–2 for the location and layout of the traverse points.

Use the same minimum of 40 traverse points for circular ducts and 42 points for rectangular ducts that were used in the alternative measurement procedure for future sampling and velocity measurements.
Install the CEMS.
Prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in section 8.1 of this method.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Determine whether effluent stratification exists using a dual probe system.
Calculate the RD only if paired samples are taken using RM 30B, RM 29, or ASTM D6784–16.
Use these procedures to certify your PEMS after initial installation and periodically thereafter to ensure the PEMS is operating properly.
Test the diluent component of your PEMS if it contains a diluent (O2 or CO2) measuring component.
Conduct a quarterly audit of the PEMS against a portable analyzer or a RM for a specified number of runs.
Perform a RA test at least once every four calendar quarters after the initial certification test.
Conduct the applicable ongoing tests listed in this section.
Conduct a Weekly System Integrity Check using a single mid- or high-level oxidized Hg reference gas.
Assess the transport and measurement of oxidized mercury.
Calculate the absolute value of the difference between the Hg CEMS output response and the reference gas.
Use Method 25 or 25A of appendix A–7 to 40 CFR part 60 to determine total gaseous organic matter concentration.
Submit notice of the intended test method to the Administrator for approval along with notification of the performance test required under § 63.7(b).
Select the sampling port location and the number/location of traverse points at the inlet and outlet of the control device.
Measure the O2 at the inlet and outlet of the control device.

preconditions.text: The alternative measurement site selection procedure may be used to determine the rotation angles in lieu of the procedure outlined in section 11.4.
The alternative procedure applies to sources where measurement locations are less than 2 equivalent or duct diameters downstream or less than one-half duct diameter upstream from a flow disturbance.
The alternative should be limited to ducts larger than 24 inches in diameter where blockage and wall effects are minimal.
Use method 25A if any of the conditions described in paragraphs (e)(1)(vi)(A) through (D) of this section apply to the control device.
